description: The practice of working together toward shared goals with coordinated, harmonious effort. It is the smooth interplay of different roles for a common purpose.
rationale: The word begins with 'mo', a sound of mutual exchange—lips coming together just as people come together. This flows through the liquid 'la', creating the smooth coordination of shared effort. The final 'wi' adds a sense of collective energy, like many hands working as one. This three-syllable dance—from mutual recognition through flowing action to unified purpose—embodies cooperation as both a practice and a joy. The sound rolls easily off the tongue, just as true cooperation makes difficult tasks feel effortless when shared.
